name: Soft-Shell Crabs with Creamy Tomato Sauce
recipeIngredient: 35 ounces Tomatoes, whole, packed in juice, drained and chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large nonreactive skillet, heat 2 tablespoons of olive oil over moderate heat for 2 minutes.
2. Add the garlic and shallots and cook, stirring frequently, until the shallots are translucent and the garlic begins to brown, about 5 to 6 minutes (do not let the garlic get too brown or it will be bitter).
3. Stir in the cumin, celery seeds, red pepper, and 1/2 teaspoon each of salt and black pepper. Cook for 1 minute longer.
4. Stir in the sugar and tomatoes, then bring to a boil. Reduce the heat to moderately low and simmer the sauce for 15 minutes.
5. Add the crème fraîche and simmer for an additional 6 minutes. Cover and keep warm.
- The sauce can be made up to 1 day ahead. Cover and refrigerate. Reheat before serving.
6. Preheat the oven to 250°F.
7. In a plate, toss the flour with the remaining 1/2 teaspoon each of salt and black pepper.
8. Pat the crabs dry with paper towels and dredge them in the seasoned flour.
9. In a large skillet, preferably nonstick, heat 2 tablespoons of olive oil over moderately high heat.
10. Add 4 crabs and cook, turning once, until red and crisp, about 3 minutes per side. Transfer the crabs to a heatproof platter and place in the oven to keep warm.
11. Repeat with the remaining 2 tablespoons of olive oil and 4 crabs.
12. To serve, spoon the tomato sauce onto warmed plates and arrange 2 crabs on the sauce on each plate.
